commit.message:
feat: Refactor homepage with Server Components, ISR, and lazy loading

- Convert homepage from 746-line client component to async Server Component
- Add ISR with 1-hour revalidation for better performance
- Implement parallel queries with Promise.all (7 sequential → 6 parallel)
- Add lazy loading for category sections with IntersectionObserver
- Create reusable homepage components:
  - AnimatedSection: GSAP scroll animation wrapper
  - HeroBranding: Static MATER letters
  - HeroCarousel: Interactive carousel with autoplay
  - ArticleSection: Reusable article grid
  - IntervjuTjedna: Featured interview section
  - LazyCategorySection: Lazy-loaded category with skeleton
  - NewsletterSection: Email signup form
  - VideoPlayer: YouTube/Vimeo/native video support
- Add styled 404 page in Mater design style
- Update category/article pages to use global 404
- Fix Intervju section to use consistent h-screen dimensions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: zone management, validation trees & admin quality dashboard

Teacher dashboard:
- Zone cards are now clickable → /teacher/zone/[zoneId] detail view
- Edit zone inline (category, description, group) and delete zone with confirmation
- Validation indicator per zone: X/Y validated, red if incomplete, green if done

Teacher zone detail (/teacher/zone/[zoneId]):
- Lists all trees with species, measurements, health, PlantNet confidence %
- Shows validation progress (required = 1 per 10 trees)
- "Validate random tree" button picks an unvalidated tree at random

Teacher validation (/teacher/validate/[treeId]):
- Shows student's measurements as read-only reference
- Teacher enters their own independent measurements
- Calculates accuracy % per field (height, crown NS/EW) using |student-teacher|/teacher
- Saves to new tree_validations table with accuracy_pct
- Result screen shows visual comparison with accuracy bars

PlantNet confidence:
- species_confidence now saved to trees table when student accepts a PlantNet suggestion
- Displayed in zone detail and admin explorer

Admin panel:
- Explore tab: drill-down browser schools → zones → trees (read-only)
- Quality tab: table of all schools with measurement accuracy, PlantNet confidence, validation progress
- Sortable by worst accuracy first, best PlantNet, most trees, or name

SQL migration 002_validation.sql adds:
- trees.species_confidence (float, 0-100)
- tree_validations table

commit.message:
Initial commit: Plivo Pub/Sub System

A real-time publish-subscribe messaging system built with FastAPI and WebSockets.

Features:
- WebSocket endpoint for publish/subscribe/unsubscribe/ping
- REST APIs for topic management and health checks
- Thread-safe with asyncio locks
- Backpressure handling for slow consumers
- Heartbeat support

commit.message:
Rufus v1.7.1: golden set tracking + data quality fix

- Track initial pills as "golden set" — after page refresh, only skip
  seeds already explored (not all completed questions)
- Pre-click pill harvest to distinguish initial vs follow-up questions
- Reset verification: ≥60% overlap check confirms Rufus actually reset
- Smart stop: exits when all initial seeds have been explored
- Improved cleanExtractedText() to strip Amazon UI junk, scripts,
  product recommendation HTML, "{} Customer question" prefixes
- Added isValidQAPair() to filter garbage entries (Rufus UI text,
  AmazonUIPageJS artifacts, too-short Q&A)
- Fixes infinite refresh loop where completedQuestions blocked all
  initial pills after page refresh

Version check runs automatically for all clients (Claude Code, Codex,
OpenCode, Gemini) by piggybacking on `relay health` — no agent
compliance with SKILL.md instructions needed.

- New: scripts/version-check.sh (cached 24h, semver compare)

commit.message:
fix: include generated media in run output regardless of store_media setting (#6793)

## Summary

Decouples `store_media` from in-run media availability. Previously,
`store_media=False` prevented media from appearing in both the DB
**and** the returned `RunOutput`, making it impossible to forward
generated images/videos/audio to external services (WhatsApp, Slack)
immediately after a run.

Now `store_media` controls **only DB persistence**. Generated media is
always available on the returned `RunOutput` for the caller to act on.

Closes #5101

---

## Bugs Fixed

This PR fixes **4 distinct bugs** in the media pipeline:

### Bug 1: `store_media=False` strips media from RunOutput

`store_media_util()` — which copies media from `ModelResponse` to
`RunOutput` — was gated behind `if agent.store_media:` in all 10 run
paths (4 agent + 6 team). When `store_media=False`, media was never
placed on `RunOutput`, so callers couldn't forward images to
WhatsApp/Slack.

**Fix:** Remove the gate. `store_media_util()` always runs. DB scrubbing
happens later in `cleanup_and_store`.

### Bug 2: Streaming images gated on `store_media` (asymmetry)

In `handle_model_response_chunk`, only images were gated behind `if
agent.store_media:`. Videos, audio, and files were never gated. This
meant streaming with `store_media=False` dropped images but not other
media types.

**Fix:** Remove the gate. All media types are now consistent — always
collected into `run_response` during streaming.

### Bug 3: Session cache aliasing leaks media back to DB

After `cleanup_and_store` scrubbed media and stored the run,
`session.upsert_run(run=run_response)` stored a **reference** to the
same `RunOutput` object. If media was later restored on that object
(e.g., via `try/finally`), subsequent `save_session()` calls would
re-persist the old run with restored media — leaking it to DB despite
`store_media=False`.

**Fix:** `cleanup_and_store` now operates on `storage_copy =
copy.copy(run_response)` — the original `run_response` is never mutated,
so the caller always sees media and the session cache holds an
independent scrubbed snapshot. Credit to @kausmeows for the copy-first
approach.

### Bug 4: `scrub_media_from_run_output` missed top-level output fields

`scrub_media_from_run_output()` scrubbed input and message media but
didn't null the top-level output fields (`images`, `videos`, `audio`,
`files`). This caused **team member responses** and **workflow executor
runs** to leak media to DB when `store_media=False`, because
`_scrub_member_responses` and `_store_executor_response` delegate to
this function.

**Fix:** Add 4 lines to null top-level output fields. Safe because
`cleanup_and_store` uses `storage_copy` (caller's original is
untouched).

---

## Full Data Lifecycle

```
BEFORE (store_media=False):

  Model generates image
        |
        v
  store_media_util()  --- if store_media: --- SKIPPED
        |                                       |
        v                                       v
  RunOutput.images = []           Media never reaches RunOutput
        |
        v
  cleanup_and_store()
        |
        +-- scrub input/messages --> DB gets scrubbed input
        |
        +-- upsert_run(run_response) --> session holds REFERENCE
        |
        +-- save_session() --> DB gets no media
        |
        v
  Caller gets RunOutput.images = None   <-- can't forward to WhatsApp/Slack


AFTER (store_media=False):

  Model generates image
        |
        v
  store_media_util()  --- ALWAYS runs --> RunOutput.images = [img]
        |
        v
  cleanup_and_store()
        |
        +-- storage_copy = copy.copy(run_response)  <-- original never mutated
        |
        +-- scrub_run_output_for_storage(storage_copy)
        |       |
        |       +-- scrub input/message media
        |       +-- null images/videos/audio/files
        |
        +-- upsert_run(storage_copy) --> session holds SCRUBBED COPY
        |
        +-- save_session() --> DB gets no media
        |
        v
  Caller gets RunOutput.images = [img]   <-- forwards to WhatsApp/Slack


TEAM MEMBER RESPONSES (store_media=False on member):

  Member agent generates image
        |
        v
  store_media_util() --> member RunOutput.images = [img]
        |
        v
  _scrub_member_responses()
        |
        +-- scrub_run_output_for_storage(member, member_response)
        |       |
        |       +-- scrub_media_from_run_output(member_response)
        |       |       |
        |       |       +-- null input/message media
        |       |       +-- null images/videos/audio/files  <-- Bug 4 fix
        |       |
        |       +-- (permanent scrub, no restore)
        |
        v
  Team session saved --> member media NOT in DB
```

---

## Files Changed

| File | Changes | What |
|------|---------|------|
| `agent/_response.py` | +6 -6 | Remove `store_media` gate on streaming
image collection (Bug 2) |
| `agent/_run.py` | +28 -23 | Remove `store_media` gates in 4 run paths
(Bug 1); copy-first `storage_copy` pattern in `cleanup_and_store` +
`acleanup_and_store` (Bug 3) |
| `team/_run.py` | +29 -25 | Same decoupling across 6 team run paths
(Bug 1); copy-first `storage_copy` in `_cleanup_and_store` +
`_acleanup_and_store` (Bug 3) |
| `utils/agent.py` | +7 -0 | Null top-level output media fields in
`scrub_media_from_run_output` (Bug 4) |
| `tests/unit/agent/test_store_media_run_output.py` | +192 | 8 tests:
sync/async x non-streaming/streaming x yield_run_output/stream_events |
| `tests/unit/agent/test_store_media_scrub_leak.py` | +218 | 10 tests:
scrub function behavior, member response scrubbing, session cache
isolation |
| `tests/unit/os/routers/test_slack_store_media.py` | +450 | 7 tests:
Slack integration — non-streaming + streaming with mock and real Agent |

---

## Test Coverage (25 tests)

**Agent core (8 tests)** — `test_store_media_run_output.py`
- sync/async x non-streaming/streaming x yield_run_output/stream_events
- Regression: `store_media=True` still works

**Scrub + DB isolation (10 tests)** — `test_store_media_scrub_leak.py`
- `scrub_media_from_run_output` nulls all 4 media types
(images/videos/audio/files)
- Works on both `RunOutput` and `TeamRunOutput`
- Member response scrubbing via `scrub_run_output_for_storage`
- `store_media=True` preserves media (regression guard)
- `cleanup_and_store` / `acleanup_and_store` never mutate the original
(copy-first)
- Session cache does NOT contain media when `store_media=False`

**Slack integration (7 tests)** — `test_slack_store_media.py`
- Non-streaming: mock agent, real Agent with `store_media=False`
- Streaming: completion chunks, content chunks with multi-image
- Real Agent streaming with `store_media=False`
- Regression: `store_media=True` still uploads

---

## Design Decisions

**Why `copy.copy()` instead of `deepcopy`?**
Shallow copy is sufficient because we only rebind top-level fields on
the copy (`storage_copy.images = None` doesn't affect
`run_response.images`). Deep copy would unnecessarily clone large media
payloads. Input/message media IS shared between the copy and original —
this is acceptable because `scrub_media_from_run_output` mutates nested
message objects in-place, which is the same behavior as pre-PR code.

**Why no explicit nulling block after `scrub_run_output_for_storage`?**
`scrub_run_output_for_storage()` calls `scrub_media_from_run_output()`
which already nulls `images/videos/audio/files`. An earlier version had
a redundant defensive block — removed because: (1) it duplicated the
scrub function's work, (2) it couldn't serve as a fallback since both
were in the same `try` block, and (3) it obscured the single source of
truth for what gets scrubbed.

**Interaction with `_scrub_member_responses`:**
For team member responses, `scrub_media_from_run_output` is called
**permanently** (no save/restore). The top-level nulling fix (Bug 4) is
critical here — without it, member-generated images leak to DB even when
the member has `store_media=False`.

---

## Related Issues

| Issue | Problem | How this PR helps |
|-------|---------|-------------------|
| **#5101** (closes) | `store_media=False` strips media from RunOutput —
can't forward images to WhatsApp | Media now stays on RunOutput for
immediate forwarding |
| **#6529** | DB bloat from inline base64 media (165MB sessions, 4GB+
tables) | Users who set `store_media=False` to avoid bloat can now still
act on media in the current run |
| **#5960** | Expired media URLs in history break subsequent LLM calls
(404 errors) | Reinforces "act now, don't persist" pattern — forward
immediately, don't rely on stored URLs |
| **#5838** | Session storage growth "extremely exaggerated" in Team
mode | Users combining `store_media=False` + `store_tool_messages=False`
now get working media without DB growth |

---

## Type of change

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] Model update
- [ ] Other:

## Checklist

- [x] Code complies with style guidelines
- [x] Ran format/validation scripts (`./scripts/format.sh` and
`./scripts/validate.sh`)
- [x] Self-review completed
- [x] Documentation updated (comments, docstrings)
- [ ] Examples and guides: Relevant cookbook examples have been included
or updated (if applicable)
- [x] Tested in clean environment
- [x] Tests added/updated (25 new tests)

---------

Co-authored-by: Mustafa Esoofally <coolmusta@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Kaustubh <shuklakaustubh84@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Code quality: fix XSS, double-send bug, timer leak, DRY cleanup

- Fix XSS: escape HTML in message innerHTML (LLM output could inject tags)
- Fix double-send: clear finalTranscript in stopRecording before onend fires
- Fix timer leak: playAudioBuffer now clears timeout via done() guard
- Fix audio interrupts: use source.stop() instead of destroying AudioContext
- Extract _meta_event() helper (was repeated 4x in SSE generator)
- Remove unused Python split_sentences (only used client-side)
- Move numpy import to top level (was deferred in hot path)
- Cache get_config_model() by file mtime (avoid re-parsing on every call)
- Replace duplicated audio IIFE with initAudio() call

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add streaming output, background tools, pipelines, SIMD arg parsing

1. Streaming: execute_stream trait method with HRTB callback, shell
   streams stdout line-by-line, http streams response chunks
2. Background: & suffix spawns tool via tokio::spawn, /tasks command
   shows status, TaskTable with Arc<Mutex<>> for cross-task access
3. Pipelines: | splits tool commands, output flows left-to-right,
   inject_pipe_data maps piped output to correct tool parameter
4. SIMD arg tokenizer: byte_classifier pass finds whitespace boundaries,
   reusable buffer via ArgTokenizer::with_capacity, replaces splitn

129 tests, zero hot path regression (23.5B insns, 3.75 IPC, 4.9 KB)

Deux ajouts dans srcSpecificT (translateGemini) et srcSpecific_t (translateManual) :
1. Short JP exclamations (すごい, あー, パンパン, etc.) = expressions communes,
   toujours traduire, jamais traiter comme noms propres.
2. Hangul dans source JP = erreur Groq (speech recognition) — traduire
   phonétiquement plutôt que garder intact.
Idem synchro prompts.js benchmark.

Basé sur benchmark JP yyy : DSK BRUT laissait 38 CJK + 70 Hangul résiduel.

commit.message:
fix: 각 도서별로 대출 가능 도서관 표시하도록 프롬프트 개선

사용자가 여러 도서를 검색했을 때 각 도서별로 대출 가능한 도서관을
개별적으로 표시하도록 시스템 프롬프트를 수정합니다.

문제:
- 여러 도서 검색 시 대출 가능 도서관이 전체 합쳐져서 표시됨
- 사용자는 각 도서별로 어디서 대출 가능한지 알고 싶어함

해결:
- 시스템 프롬프트에 "각 도서마다 GetLibrariesWithBook 호출" 지침 추가
- 응답 형식을 "각 도서별로 표시"로 명확화
- 예시 형식에 각 도서 아래 대출 가능/불가 도서관 표시

변경 파일:
- AiOrchestrationService.java: 시스템 프롬프트 개선
- ToolBasedAiOrchestrationService.java: 시스템 프롬프트 개선

기대 효과:
1. *도서명* (ISBN: xxx)
   대출 가능한 도서관: ✅ 도서관명
   대출 불가능한 도서관: ❌ 도서관명
2. *도서명* (ISBN: xxx)
   대출 가능한 도서관: ✅ 도서관명
   ...

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ingestion): distinguish infra vs message errors in dead-letter logic (#186) (#190)

The ingestion worker now classifies exceptions into infrastructure errors
(DB down, missing tables, connection failures) and message-level errors
(bad data, validation failures). Infrastructure errors raise
InfrastructureError and exit the worker process for ECS restart, leaving
messages unacknowledged in the Redis stream. Only message-level errors
are dead-lettered after retry exhaustion.

Adds a health_check() method that verifies DB connectivity and required
tables exist before the worker starts consuming messages.

commit.message:
test: add expected failure support to node_compat test harness (#32451)

## Summary

Extends the node_compat test harness to support **expected failures** —
tests that are known to fail with a specific exit code and/or output
pattern. This avoids disabling tests entirely when we know they fail in
a predictable way.

### Changes

- **`PlatformExpectation` enum** — Platform fields (`windows`, `darwin`,
`linux`) now accept `false` (skip), `true` (expect pass), or `{
"exitCode": N, "output": "pattern" }` (expected failure)
- **`ExpectedFailure` struct** — Describes expected exit code and/or
output pattern with `[WILDCARD]` support
- **`handle_expected_failure()`** — Reports pass when failure matches
expectations, fails when test unexpectedly passes or fails differently
- **`tests/node_compat/schema.json`** — JSON Schema for `config.jsonc`
enabling editor validation and autocompletion
- **Unit tests** — Comprehensive deserialization and logic tests for all
config patterns

### Config examples

```jsonc
// Skip on a platform
"test.js": { "windows": false, "reason": "not supported" }

// Expected failure on one platform
"test.js": { "windows": { "exitCode": 1, "output": "error [WILDCARD]" } }

// Expected failure on all platforms
"test.js": { "exitCode": 1, "output": "error [WILDCARD]" }

// Mixed: passes on linux/darwin, expected failure on windows
"test.js": {
  "linux": true,
  "darwin": true,
  "windows": { "exitCode": 1, "output": "Failed [WILDCARD]" }
}
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: bartlomieju <13602871+bartlomieju@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Bartek Iwańczuk <biwanczuk@gmail.com>

commit.message:
fix(cli): force-exit after analyze to prevent KuzuDB hang (#192)

KuzuDB's native module holds open handles that prevent Node.js from
exiting cleanly. Previously only force-exited when embeddings were used
(for ONNX Runtime segfault workaround), but the same issue affects all
analyze runs. Now always calls process.exit(0) after completion.

fix: permission flyout viewport overflow + app-scope context (#410) (#473)

## Summary
- Fix permission info flyout getting clipped off-screen when opened on
bottom plugins
- Add app-scope cross-project context note and rewritten descriptions
for app-scoped plugins with cross-project permissions

Closes #410

## Changes
### `PluginListSettings.tsx` — `PermissionInfoPopup`
- **Viewport overflow fix**: On open, check if the popup would overflow
the bottom of the viewport using an estimated height constant. If so,
position the popup *above* the button (using CSS `bottom`) instead of
below (using CSS `top`).
- **App-scope context note**: When the plugin is `scope: 'app'` and has
cross-project permissions (`agent-config.cross-project` or
`workspace.cross-project`), show a `ctp-peach` warning note explaining
that cross-project access is implicit and does not require per-project
enablement.
- **Description rewriting**: For app-scoped plugins, replace the
misleading "where the plugin is also enabled" wording in cross-project
permission descriptions with "across all projects (app-scoped — no
bilateral consent required)".

### `PluginPermissions.test.tsx`
- Test: app-scope cross-project note is shown for app-scoped plugins
- Test: app-scope cross-project note is NOT shown for project-scoped
plugins
- Test: cross-project descriptions are rewritten for app-scoped plugins
- Test: popup flips above button when near viewport bottom

## Test Plan
- [x] Existing permission popup tests still pass (open, close, toggle,
descriptions, fixed positioning)
- [x] New test: app-scoped plugin with cross-project perms shows the
warning note
- [x] New test: project-scoped plugin does NOT show the warning note
- [x] New test: description text is rewritten for app-scoped
cross-project permissions
- [x] New test: popup uses `bottom` positioning when button is near
viewport bottom
- [x] Full suite: 5414/5414 tests pass
- [x] TypeScript: `tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] Lint: `eslint .` clean

## Manual Validation
1. Open Settings > Plugins with an app-scoped plugin that has
`agent-config.cross-project` or `workspace.cross-project` permissions
2. Click the (i) icon — verify the peach warning note appears and
descriptions mention "across all projects (app-scoped...)"
3. Do the same for a project-scoped plugin — verify no warning note,
original descriptions shown
4. Scroll down so the last plugin is near the bottom of the viewport,
click its (i) icon — verify the popup renders above the button instead
of being clipped

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: preserve expanded folder children during file tree refresh (#472)

## Summary
- Fix bug where expanding a folder in the file plugin would briefly show
contents then hide them, leaving the folder icon in an expanded state
but with no visible children
- Root cause: `loadTree()` replaced the entire tree with shallow
`depth:1` data, wiping lazily-loaded children while the `expanded` Set
still marked folders as open
- Add 17 new tests covering folder expansion stability and file
structure rendering

## Changes
- **`mergeTreePreservingChildren()`** — new helper that merges a shallow
tree refresh with the existing tree, preserving lazily-loaded children
of expanded folders so they don't flicker or disappear
- **`updateNodeChildren()`** — extracted helper for updating a specific
node's children in the tree (also simplifies `toggleExpand`)
- **`loadTree()` modified** — uses merge instead of replace, then
re-fetches expanded folder children in parallel to keep content fresh
without visual disruption
- **`expandedRef`** — ref used to access current expanded state inside
`loadTree` without adding it to the dependency array (avoids unnecessary
effect re-registrations)

## Test Plan
- [x] Expanded folder children survive a `loadTree` refresh
- [x] File watcher refresh preserves expanded folder children
- [x] Multiple expanded folders all survive refresh
- [x] Collapsed folder stays collapsed after refresh
- [x] File watcher debounces rapid changes into one refresh
- [x] Root-level directories and files render correctly
- [x] Directories hidden until expanded (lazy load pattern)
- [x] Expanding a folder lazy-loads and shows children
- [x] Collapsing a folder hides its children
- [x] Nested folder expansion (parent → child)
- [x] Collapsing parent hides all nested children
- [x] Expand-collapse-expand cycle re-shows children
- [x] Expand All shows all nested directory contents
- [x] Collapse All hides all expanded children
- [x] Empty tree shows "No files found" placeholder
- [x] Keyboard Enter on directory toggles expansion
- [x] Nested expanded folders survive refresh
- [x] Typecheck passes, lint clean, all 5427 tests pass

## Manual Validation
1. Open the file plugin sidebar
2. Expand a folder — children should appear and stay visible
3. Make an external file change (e.g., save in another editor) —
expanded folder should retain its children
4. Click Refresh — expanded folders should keep their children
5. Expand multiple folders, then refresh — all should persist
6. Collapse a folder, refresh — should stay collapsed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial release: MicroPython CSI module for ESP32-S3

Full WiFi Channel State Information access via ESP-IDF's
esp_wifi_set_csi_rx_cb() API, exposed as a MicroPython user C module.

- Captures LLTF, HTLTF and STBC-HTLTF2 raw I/Q data
- All rx_ctrl metadata available (RSSI, channel, MCS, bandwidth, …)
- Polling (csi.read) with GIL release and async callback mode
- 16-frame ring buffer, drop-oldest strategy
- parse_iq() helper for (I, Q) tuple conversion
- Tested with MicroPython v1.24.1 + ESP-IDF 5.2.2 on ESP32-S3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Switch live instruments to EURUSD+NZDUSD+EURGBP based on 7-pair backtest

Backtest of 7 pairs (10K H1 bars, £700 equity) showed:
- NZDUSD: PF 3.25, +71.1p (best of all pairs)
- EURGBP: PF 0.91, +10.3p (adds volume)
- GBPUSD: PF 0.46, -119.6p (removed - consistently negative)

Added pip values for NZDUSD, USDCAD, USDCHF, EURGBP.
Added run_bt_pairs.py multi-pair backtest script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: 完整实现 TOTP 动态验证码功能 (#11)

* feat: 完整实现 TOTP 动态验证码功能

核心功能：
- 添加 TOTP 生成与实时刷新（基于 RFC 6238）
- 支持三套 UI（移动端、桌面端、快捷面板）
- 列表页面显示 TOTP 图标标识
- 详情页面显示验证码与倒计时
- 一键复制验证码，30 秒自动清空剪贴板

添加方式：
- 移动端：相机扫描 + 选择图片识别 QR 码
- 桌面端：相机扫描 + 选择图片识别 QR 码
- 手动输入 Secret Key（支持自动格式化）

图片识别增强：
- 多种识别策略（缩放、对比度增强、灰度化）
- 两种二值化算法（HybridBinarizer、GlobalHistogramBinarizer）
- 支持随手拍的照片（二维码周围有其他内容）

技术实现：
- 数据结构扩展（totpSecret、totpIssuer）
- AES-256-GCM 加密存储
- 性能优化（不阻塞 UI 线程）
- 完整国际化支持（中英文）

commit.message:
feat: prove raw block content and block position advance theorems (#677)

* feat: prove raw block content and block position advance theorems

Add three new Zstd block-level spec theorems:
- decompressRawBlock_pos_eq: raw block advances position by blockSize
- decompressRLEBlock_pos_eq: RLE block advances position by 1
- decompressRawBlock_content: raw block copies input bytes verbatim

These are characterizing properties needed for future decompressBlocks
loop correctness proofs — they specify exactly how each block type
consumes input, complementing the existing size and content theorems.
